title: Aerosol generating device and system
abstract: There is provided an aerosol generating device (100), comprising a consumable chamber (120) for receiving a consumable (200); an IR heating system (130) configured to emit IR radiation into the consumable chamber; wherein the IR heating system is configured to emit IR radiation with a wavenumber in a first wavenumber range or a second wavenumber range, wherein the first wavenumber range is 3600 to 2900 inverse-centimetres, the second wavenumber range is 1700 to 800 inverse-centimetres.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. A aerosol generating device (100), comprising: a consumable chamber (120) for receiving a consumable (200); an IR heating system (130) configured to emit into the consumable chamber IR radiation with a wavenumber in a first wavenumber range or a second wavenumber range, wherein the first wavenumber range is 3600 to 2900 inverse-centimetres, the second wavenumber range is 1700 to 800 inverse-centimetres. 2. The aerosol generating device according to claim 1 , wherein the second wavenumber range is 1500 to 1200 inverse-centimetres, and preferably 1450 to 1300 inverse-centimetres. 3. The aerosol generating device according to claim 1 , wherein the second wavenumber range is 1200 to 900 inverse-centimetres, and preferably 1100 to 1000 inverse-centimetres. 4. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the first wavenumber range is 3500 to 3000 inverse-centimetres, and preferably 3400 to 3200 inverse-centimetres. 5. The aerosol generating device according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the first wavenumber range is 3000 to 2900 inverse-centimetres. 6. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein an emission spectrum of the IR heating system has a local maximum at a wavenumber in the first wavenumber range or the second wavenumber range. 7. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the IR radiation has a distribution of wavenumbers including a first wavenumber in the first wavenumber range, and a second wavenumber in the second wavenumber range. 8. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the IR heating system is configured to generate IR radiation with wavenumbers exclusively in the first wavenumber range or the second wavenumber range. 9. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the IR radiation has a continuous spectrum of wavenumbers across the first wavenumber range or the second wavenumber range. 10. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the IR heating system is configured for heating the consumable to a temperature of 180 to 250 degrees Celsius, preferably 180 degrees Celsius to 235 degrees Celsius, and more preferably 180 degrees Celsius to 220 degrees Celsius. 11. The aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, wherein the IR heating system comprises an electrothermal element (132) configured to generate the IR radiation. 12. The aerosol generating device according to claim 11 , wherein the electrothermal element is a nanocoating applied to a chamber body (134) enclosing the consumable chamber. 13. The aerosol generating device according to claim 11 or 12, wherein a plurality of electrothermal elements (134) is provided. 14. The aerosol generating device according to claim 13, wherein the plurality of electrothermal elements comprises a first set of electrothermal elements (134) and a second set of electrothermal elements (134), wherein each electrothermal element of the first set is configured to generate IR radiation having a first set of wavenumbers, and each electrothermal element of the second set is configured to generate IR radiation having a second set of wavenumbers, and wherein the first set and the second set of electrothermal elements are alternatingly arranged about the consumable chamber. 15. An aerosol generating system comprising an aerosol generating device according to any preceding claim, further comprising a consumable (200), wherein an absorbance spectrum of the consumable has a local maximum in the first wavenumber range or the second wavenumber range.
